```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  lambda <- sample(seq(0.5,5,0.5),1); x <- sample(0:7,1)
  ans <- lambda/(x+1)
  vals <- round(c(ans,(x+1)/lambda,lambda/(x+2),lambda,x+1),4)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Se $X\sim\operatorname{Poisson}(\lambda)$, com $\lambda=`r lambda`$, qual é a razão $p_X(`r x+1`)/p_X(`r x`)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
Pela pmf de Poisson,
$$\frac{p(x+1)}{p(x)}
=\frac{\lambda^{x+1}/(x+1)!}{\lambda^x/x!}
=\frac{\lambda}{x+1}
=`r sprintf("%.4f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S6Q9_recorrencia_Poisson
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
